title: Valentine flow animations, final Yes celebration page, and transparent generated assets

requirements.summary: Add consistent on-enter and cute per-page animations across the Valentine step pages without breaking the hearts-burst flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Each step/page visibly animates in when it is shown (e.g., fade/scale/slide-in on the main card content).
Animations are consistent with the existing Valentine theme and do not block form inputs or navigation.
Existing slider-completion hearts burst animation still works and still auto-navigates to the main Valentine page.

file_operations.description: Add/extend global Valentine-themed animation utilities (e.g., a step on-enter animation for main card/content and a couple of cute accent animations) to be reused by the step pages.
Apply the new on-enter transition class to the main Card/content wrapper and add a small page-specific cute accent animation while keeping inputs/buttons fully usable.
Apply the new on-enter transition class to the main Card/content wrapper and add a small page-specific cute accent animation while keeping inputs/buttons fully usable.
Apply the new on-enter transition class to the main Card/content wrapper while ensuring the existing HeartsBurstOverlay animation still triggers and completes navigation exactly as before.
Apply the new on-enter transition class to the main Card/content wrapper and add a small page-specific cute accent animation consistent with the Valentine theme.
Apply the new on-enter transition class to the main Card/content wrapper and add a small page-specific cute accent animation while preserving the Go Back behavior.

requirements.summary: Replace the Yes alert with navigation to a new final celebration page within the existing App.tsx step flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the "Yes" button on the main Valentine page routes to a new final celebration page within the existing App.tsx step flow (no alert is shown).
The final page contains celebratory Valentine-themed UI and at least one clear action (e.g., restart/go back).
The existing "No" flow still navigates to the WrongAnswerPage and the Go Back button still returns to the main Valentine page.

file_operations.description: Create a new final confirmation/celebration page with Valentine-themed UI and at least one clear action (e.g., Restart and/or Back to the main question), using the existing layout and read-only UI components.

Extend the local step-state flow to include a new final celebration step; wire navigation from the main page's Yes action to this new step; also wire actions from the final page to restart and/or return to the main step.
Remove the browser alert on Yes; accept an onYes callback prop from App.tsx and call it when the user presses "Yes"; keep the existing No flow intact.

requirements.summary: Ensure all generated PNG assets used by the UI have transparent backgrounds, including the background pattern image if it is used.
acceptanceCriteria: All PNG assets under /assets/generated used by the UI render without a visible non-transparent box/background around the illustrated elements (where applicable).
If the background image (valentine-bg.dim_1920x1080.png) is used, it has transparency so the existing gradient overlay can show through cleanly.

file_operations.description: Replace/update the generated asset to ensure it has a fully transparent background/alpha channel (no opaque box), while keeping the same filename and location for static serving.
Replace/update the generated asset to ensure it has a fully transparent background/alpha channel (no opaque box), while keeping the same filename and location for static serving.
Replace/update the generated asset to ensure it has a fully transparent background/alpha channel (no opaque box), while keeping the same filename and location for static serving.
Confirm the background image usage remains compatible with transparent PNGs (gradient overlay remains visible and readable) and keep using static assets under frontend/public/assets/generated.
